citeinfo.title: Physiographic divisions of the conterminous U. S.

descript.abstract: This is a polygon coverage of Physiographic Divisions in the conterminous United States. It was automated from Fenneman's 1:7,000,000-scale map, "Physical Divisions of the United States," which is based on eight major 1946 divisions, 25 provinces, and 86 sections representing distinctive areas having common topography, rock types and structure, and geologic and geomorphic history.
descript.purpose: The primary use of this data is for regional and national data display rather than specific local data analysis due to the small scale (1:7,000,000) of this coverage.

idinfo.useconst: The scale of the data (1:7,000,000) limits its use to broad overviews of physiographic divisions.

citeinfo.origin: Fenneman, Nevin M., and Johnson, Douglas W.
citeinfo.title: Physical divisions of the United States

procstep.procdesc:
This coverage was automated from Fenneman's 1:7,000,000-scale map, "Physical Divisions of the United States," 1946. Features were digitized from a stable mylar base into the United States boundary coverage. Since the map is in polyconic projection, and the U.S. boundary coverage was in Albers Equal Area Projection, the U.S. boundary coverage was first projected into polyconic projection before digitizing.
Once digitizing was complete, the coverage was projected back into Albers Equal Area Projection, with the following parameters:
First parallel 29 30 00 N Second parallel 45 30 00 N Central meridian -96 00 00 W Latitude of projection origin 23 00 00 N False easting 0.0 False northing 0.0
Fennemans map is missing three boundaries separating:
1. 5a/5b (which separates the Northern and Southern sections of Blue Ridge)
2. 6a/6b (which separates Tennessee and Middle sections of Ridge and Valley)
3. 24b/24c (which separates Olympic Mountains and Oregon Coast Range of the Pacific Border province)
These boundaries were researched, then merged into the coverage.
Added attributes DIVISION, PROVINCE and SECTION to the polygon attribute table.

A user reported that section 23c, Southern Cascade Mountain Section was missing. Added boundary between Middle Cascade Mountain section (23b) and Southern Cascade Mountain Section (23c). Added label for Southern Cascade Mountain Section. Added annotation for 23c, Southern Cascade Mountain Section.

overview.eaover:
DATAFILE NAME: PHYSIO.PAT 9 ITEMS: STARTING IN POSITION 1 COL ITEM NAME WDTH OPUT TYP N.DEC ALTERNATE NAME 1 AREA 4 12 F 3 5 PERIMETER 4 12 F 3 9 PHYSIO# 4 4 B - 13 PHYSIO-ID 4 5 B - 17 FCODE 3 5 I - 20 FENCODE 4 5 C - 24 DIVISION 23 23 C - 47 PROVINCE 24 24 C - 71 SECTION 29 29 C - 100 PROVCODE 3 3 I -
FCODE - Numeric code for physiographic section. Values 1-243.
FENCODE - Fenneman's code (number+alpha) for physiographic section
DIVISION - Name of major physiographic division
PROVINCE - Name of physiographic province
SECTION - Name of physiographic section (see note below)
PROVCODE - Numeric code for physiographic province. Values 1, 3-25.
INFO file PHYSIO.PROVCODE contains numeric codes for provinces:
PROVINCE PROVCODE ------------------------ -------- SUPERIOR UPLAND 1 COASTAL PLAIN 3 PIEDMONT 4 BLUE RIDGE 5 VALLEY AND RIDGE 6 ST. LAWRENCE VALLEY 7 APPALACHIAN PLATEAUS 8 NEW ENGLAND 9 ADIRONDACK 10 INTERIOR LOW PLATEAUS 11 CENTRAL LOWLAND 12 GREAT PLAINS 13 OZARK PLATEAUS 14 OUACHITA 15 SOUTHERN ROCKY MOUNTAINS 16 WYOMING BASIN 17 MIDDLE ROCKY MOUNTAINS 18 NORTHERN ROCKY MOUNTAINS 19 COLUMBIA PLATEAU 20 COLORADO PLATEAUS 21 BASIN AND RANGE 22 CASCADE-SIERRA MOUNTAINS 23 PACIFIC BORDER 24 LOWER CALIFORNIAN 25
Because there are no defined physiographic section classifications for the Northern Rockies, Middle Rockies, Southern Rockies, Wyoming Basin, Superior Upland, Lower Californian and Adirondack Provinces, the SECTION field in the polygon attribute table is empty for these physiographic provinces.
FEN- DIVISION PROVINCE SECTION CODE 1 LAURENTIAN UPLAND SUPERIOR UPLAND - 3a ATLANTIC PLAIN COASTAL PLAIN EMBAYED 3b ATLANTIC PLAIN COASTAL PLAIN SEA ISLAND 3c ATLANTIC PLAIN COASTAL PLAIN FLORIDIAN 3d ATLANTIC PLAIN COASTAL PLAIN EAST GULF COASTAL PLAIN 3e ATLANTIC PLAIN COASTAL PLAIN MISSISSIPPI ALLUVIAL PLAIN 3f ATLANTIC PLAIN COASTAL PLAIN WEST GULF COASTAL PLAIN 4a APPALACHIAN HIGHLANDS PIEDMONT PIEDMONT UPLAND 4b APPALACHIAN HIGHLANDS PIEDMONT PIEDMONT LOWLANDS 5a APPALACHIAN HIGHLANDS BLUE RIDGE NORTHERN 5b APPALACHIAN HIGHLANDS BLUE RIDGE SOUTHERN 6a APPALACHIAN HIGHLANDS VALLEY AND RIDGE TENNESSEE 6b APPALACHIAN HIGHLANDS VALLEY AND RIDGE MIDDLE 6c APPALACHIAN HIGHLANDS VALLEY AND RIDGE HUDSON VALLEY 7a APPALACHIAN HIGHLANDS ST. LAWRENCE VALLEY CHAMPLAIN 8a APPALACHIAN HIGHLANDS APPALACHIAN PLATEAUS MOHAWK 8b APPALACHIAN HIGHLANDS APPALACHIAN PLATEAUS CATSKILL 8c APPALACHIAN HIGHLANDS APPALACHIAN PLATEAUS SOUTHERN NEW YORK 8d APPALACHIAN HIGHLANDS APPALACHIAN PLATEAUS ALLEGHENY MOUNTAIN 8e APPALACHIAN HIGHLANDS APPALACHIAN PLATEAUS KANAWHA 8f APPALACHIAN HIGHLANDS APPALACHIAN PLATEAUS CUMBERLAND PLATEAU 8g APPALACHIAN HIGHLANDS APPALACHIAN PLATEAUS CUMBERLAND MOUNTAIN 9a APPALACHIAN HIGHLANDS NEW ENGLAND SEABOARD LOWLAND 9b APPALACHIAN HIGHLANDS NEW ENGLAND NEW ENGLAND UPLAND 9c APPALACHIAN HIGHLANDS NEW ENGLAND WHITE MOUNTAIN 9d APPALACHIAN HIGHLANDS NEW ENGLAND GREEN MOUNTAIN 9e APPALACHIAN HIGHLANDS NEW ENGLAND TACONIC 10 APPALACHIAN HIGHLANDS ADIRONDACK - 11a INTERIOR PLAINS INTERIOR LOW PLATEAUS HIGHLAND RIM 11b INTERIOR PLAINS INTERIOR LOW PLATEAUS LEXINGTON PLAIN 11c INTERIOR PLAINS INTERIOR LOW PLATEAUS NASHVILLE BASIN 12a INTERIOR PLAINS CENTRAL LOWLAND EASTERN LAKE 12b INTERIOR PLAINS CENTRAL LOWLAND WESTERN LAKE 12c INTERIOR PLAINS CENTRAL LOWLAND WISCONSIN DRIFTLESS 12d INTERIOR PLAINS CENTRAL LOWLAND TILL PLAINS 12e INTERIOR PLAINS CENTRAL LOWLAND DISSECTED TILL PLAINS 12f INTERIOR PLAINS CENTRAL LOWLAND OSAGE PLAINS 13k INTERIOR PLAINS GREAT PLAINS CENTRAL TEXAS 13a INTERIOR PLAINS GREAT PLAINS MISSOURI PLATEAU, GLACIATED 13b INTERIOR PLAINS GREAT PLAINS MISSOURI PLATEAU, UNGLACIAT 13c INTERIOR PLAINS GREAT PLAINS BLACK HILLS 13d INTERIOR PLAINS GREAT PLAINS HIGH PLAINS 13e INTERIOR PLAINS GREAT PLAINS PLAINS BORDER 13f INTERIOR PLAINS GREAT PLAINS COLORADO PIEDMONT 13g INTERIOR PLAINS GREAT PLAINS RATON 13h INTERIOR PLAINS GREAT PLAINS PECOS VALLEY 13i INTERIOR PLAINS GREAT PLAINS EDWARDS PLATEAU 14a INTERIOR HIGHLANDS OZARK PLATEAUS SPRINGFIELD-SALEM PLATEAUS 14b INTERIOR HIGHLANDS OZARK PLATEAUS BOSTON "MOUNTAINS" 15a INTERIOR HIGHLANDS OUACHITA ARKANSAS VALLEY 15b INTERIOR HIGHLANDS OUACHITA OUACHITA MOUNTAINS 16 ROCKY MOUNTAIN SYSTEM SOUTHERN ROCKY MOUNTAINS - 17 ROCKY MOUNTAIN SYSTEM WYOMING BASIN - 18 ROCKY MOUNTAIN SYSTEM MIDDLE ROCKY MOUNTAINS - 19 ROCKY MOUNTAIN SYSTEM NORTHERN ROCKY MOUNTAINS - 20a INTERMONTANE PLATEAUS COLUMBIA PLATEAU WALLA WALLA PLATEAU 20b INTERMONTANE PLATEAUS COLUMBIA PLATEAU BLUE MOUNTAIN 20c INTERMONTANE PLATEAUS COLUMBIA PLATEAU PAYETTE 20d INTERMONTANE PLATEAUS COLUMBIA PLATEAU SNAKE RIVER PLAIN 20e INTERMONTANE PLATEAUS COLUMBIA PLATEAU HARNEY 21a INTERMONTANE PLATEAUS COLORADO PLATEAUS HIGH PLATEAUS OF UTAH 21b INTERMONTANE PLATEAUS COLORADO PLATEAUS UINTA BASIN 21c INTERMONTANE PLATEAUS COLORADO PLATEAUS CANYON LANDS 21d INTERMONTANE PLATEAUS COLORADO PLATEAUS NAVAJO 21e INTERMONTANE PLATEAUS COLORADO PLATEAUS GRAND CANYON 21f INTERMONTANE PLATEAUS COLORADO PLATEAUS DATIL 22a INTERMONTANE PLATEAUS BASIN AND RANGE GREAT BASIN 22b INTERMONTANE PLATEAUS BASIN AND RANGE SONORAN DESERT 22c INTERMONTANE PLATEAUS BASIN AND RANGE SALTON TROUGH 22d INTERMONTANE PLATEAUS BASIN AND RANGE MEXICAN HIGHLAND 22e INTERMONTANE PLATEAUS BASIN AND RANGE SACRAMENTO 23a PACIFIC MOUNTAIN SYSTEM CASCADE-SIERRA MOUNTAINS NORTHERN CASCADE MOUNTAINS 23b PACIFIC MOUNTAIN SYSTEM CASCADE-SIERRA MOUNTAINS MIDDLE CASCADE MOUNTAINS 23c PACIFIC MOUNTAIN SYSTEM CASCADE-SIERRA MOUNTAINS SOUTHERN CASCADE MOUNTAINS 23d PACIFIC MOUNTAIN SYSTEM CASCADE-SIERRA MOUNTAINS SIERRA NEVADA 24a PACIFIC MOUNTAIN SYSTEM PACIFIC BORDER PUGET TROUGH 24b PACIFIC MOUNTAIN SYSTEM PACIFIC BORDER OLYMPIC MOUNTAINS 24c PACIFIC MOUNTAIN SYSTEM PACIFIC BORDER OREGON COAST RANGE 24d PACIFIC MOUNTAIN SYSTEM PACIFIC BORDER KLAMATH MOUNTAINS 24e PACIFIC MOUNTAIN SYSTEM PACIFIC BORDER CALIFORNIA TROUGH 24f PACIFIC MOUNTAIN SYSTEM PACIFIC BORDER CALIFORNIA COAST RANGES 24g PACIFIC MOUNTAIN SYSTEM PACIFIC BORDER LOS ANGELES RANGES 25 PACIFIC MOUNTAIN SYSTEM LOWER CALIFORNIAN -

cntaddr.address: Denver Federal Center, Building 810, Mail Stop 302

distinfo.distliab:
Although this data set has been used by the U.S. Geological Survey, U.S. Department of the Interior, no warranty expressed or implied is made by the U.S. Geological Survey as to the accuracy of the data and related materials. The act of distribution shall not constitute any such warranty, and no responsibility is assumed by the U.S. Geological Survey in the use of this data, software, or related materials.
Any use of trade, product, or firm names is for descriptive purposes only and does not imply endorsement by the U.S. Government.

metainfo.metstdn: FGDC Content Standards for Digital Geospatial Metadata
